docs.title: Claude Skills Marketplace
docs.text:
Claude skills are ready-made capabilities that extend what Claude Code can do. Instead of explaining your workflow to Claude from scratch every time, you install a skill once and invoke it with a single command.

This marketplace is where Mirantis teams share the skills they've built.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: You need Claude Code installed and running. If you haven't done that yet, ask IT — setup takes about five minutes and your Mirantis credentials are all you need.

docs.title: One-time setup
docs.text:
Add the Mirantis skills marketplace to your Claude Code installation. Open a terminal, start Claude Code, and run:

/plugin marketplace add https://jjainschigg-r.github.io/claude-skills-marketplace/marketplace.json

You only need to do this once. Claude Code will remember it.

docs.title: Browse available skills
docs.text:
To see what's available:

/plugin list

docs.title: Install a skill
docs.text:
/plugin install <skill-name>@mirantis-skills

Replace <skill-name> with the name of the skill you want. For example:

/plugin install example-skill@mirantis-skills

docs.title: Use a skill
docs.text:
Once installed, invoke a skill by typing / followed by its name:

/example-skill

Claude Code will run the skill in the context of whatever you're working on.

docs.title: Keep skills up to date
docs.text:
When new skills are added or existing ones are updated, refresh your local list:

/plugin marketplace update mirantis-skills

Then reinstall any skills you want to update.

docs.title: Contribute a skill
docs.text: Built something useful? The skills in this marketplace are maintained in a shared repository. Reach out to your team lead or open a pull request — the more skills we share, the less we each have to reinvent.

docs.title: example-mcp-node-skill
docs.text: Demonstrates a skill backed by a Node.js MCP server. When invoked, it calls a running Node.js process via the Model Context Protocol and returns a figlet ASCII art greeting. Proof of life for the Node.js MCP plugin pattern.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Node.js and npm must be installed. Dependencies are installed automatically into a persistent data directory on first run.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
If you haven't added the marketplace yet:

/plugin marketplace add https://jjainschigg-r.github.io/claude-skills-marketplace/marketplace.json

Then install the skill:

/plugin install example-mcp-node-skill@mirantis-skills

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
/example-mcp-node-skill

Claude will call the hello_node MCP tool and return a figlet ASCII art greeting.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
On install, a Node.js MCP server starts alongside Claude Code. The server declares a hello_node tool. The skill's only job is to call that tool — all the Node.js logic lives in the server, not the prompt.

A wrapper script (run-server.sh) handles dependency installation on first run, storing node_modules in ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} so they survive plugin updates. The server script is always copied fresh from the plugin on startup, so code changes take effect on reinstall.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: Version 1.0.0 Runtime Node.js via npm External dependency figlet Maintained by Mirantis

docs.title: example-mcp-python-skill
docs.text: Demonstrates a skill backed by a Python MCP server. When invoked, it calls a running Python process via the Model Context Protocol and returns a cowsay greeting. Proof of life for the Python MCP plugin pattern.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: uv must be installed. It handles Python and dependency management automatically — no separate pip install needed.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
If you haven't added the marketplace yet:

/plugin marketplace add https://jjainschigg-r.github.io/claude-skills-marketplace/marketplace.json

Then install the skill:

/plugin install example-mcp-python-skill@mirantis-skills

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
/example-mcp-python-skill

Claude will call the hello_python MCP tool and return a cowsay greeting.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
On install, a Python MCP server starts alongside Claude Code. The server declares a hello_python tool. The skill's only job is to call that tool — all the Python logic lives in the server, not the prompt.

Dependencies (mcp, cowsay) are declared inline in the script using PEP 723 and installed automatically by uv on first run.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: Version 1.0.0 Runtime Python via uv External dependency cowsay Maintained by Mirantis

docs.title: example-skill
docs.text: A placeholder skill used to validate the Mirantis Claude Skills Marketplace pipeline.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
If you haven't added the marketplace yet:

/plugin marketplace add https://jjainschigg-r.github.io/claude-skills-marketplace/marketplace.json

Then install the skill:

/plugin install example-skill@mirantis-skills

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
/example-skill

Claude will confirm that the marketplace pipeline is working correctly.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: Version 1.0.0 Maintained by Mirantis

docs.title: markdown-doc-cleaner
docs.text: Turns rough notes into a clean, well-structured Markdown document with headings and bullet points.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
If you haven't added the marketplace yet:

/plugin marketplace add https://jjainschigg-r.github.io/claude-skills-marketplace/marketplace.json

Then install the skill:

/plugin install markdown-doc-cleaner@mirantis-skills

docs.title: Use
docs.text:
/markdown-doc-cleaner

Paste or type your rough notes when prompted. Claude will reformat them into a clean Markdown document with a title, headings, and bullet points — preserving all of your original content.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: Version 1.0.0 Maintained by Mirantis

docs.title: Plugin Dev Tools
docs.text: Plugin dev tools are fully-validated skills for creating, reviewing, and submitting plugins to this marketplace. Documentation for individual tools will appear here as they are released.
